candidates.why: Appears directly beneath the selected COGS row 'Cost of services sold' and above the COGS balancing line, indicating it is a component/detail of COGS rather than a separate same-level operating expense to add later.
Listed in the COGS detail section immediately after 'Cost of services sold', so it appears embedded within the selected COGS bucket.
Shown with the other COGS-related amortization/depreciation detail lines under 'Cost of services sold', suggesting inclusion in the selected COGS row.
Presented in the same block of detail lines beneath 'Cost of services sold' and before the balancing row, so it looks like a COGS component already captured in the selected COGS total.
Also appears in the COGS detail block under 'Cost of services sold'; despite zero values, it reads as a COGS subcomponent rather than a separate add-on line.

variables.notes: The selected COGS reference bucket is 'Cost of services sold'. The visible D&A rows sit as detail lines underneath that bucket and above 'Cost of services sold - Balancing value', so the conservative interpretation is that they are already embedded in the selected COGS total. No separate same-level COGS D&A summary line should be added.

candidates.row_label: Depreciation in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses
candidates.why: Explicit SG&A-related D&A row, but it appears within the SG&A section alongside 'Selling expenses' and 'Administrative expenses' and immediately above 'Admin. Expense - Balancing value', indicating it is a component of the selected XSGA bucket rather than a separate same-level expense to add later.

A zero-valued amortization row appears in the SG&A section, but as presented it looks like a subcomponent/detail within the administrative expense area, not a separate operating line to add on top of the selected XSGA rows.

variables.notes: The selected XSGA_COMPONENTS reference bucket is the combination of 'Selling and administrative expenses', 'Selling expenses', and 'Administrative expenses'. The explicit SG&A D&A row appears to be nested within that bucket, so adding it separately would risk double counting. No separate R&D row is visible and XRD is empty, but there is no explicit 'Amortization in R&D' line to consider.
